summary: Iteration 12 backend-only verification of the new audit-log system (GET /api/admin/audit-logs, GET /api/admin/audit-logs/summary) and the DOCX analysis download (GET /api/admin/analysis-doc). Created /app/backend/tests/test_audit_and_analysis.py with 21 pytest cases. All 21 pass and full suite is 118/118 (97 prior + 21 new). Verified: (a) GM 200 + correct response shape for both audit endpoints (list returns {total,count,logs}; summary returns {total,last_24h,failed_logins_24h,by_action}); (b) 401 unauth and 403 for a fresh TEST_audit_dm_probe department_manager; (c) failed logins (bad user + wrong password for admin) are captured with action='login', status='failed'; (d) admin/admin123 success login writes action='login', status='success', entity_type='auth'; (e) action=login filter returns only login entries and entity_type=auth filter is respected; (f) full department create→update→delete cycle produces 3 audit rows with entity_type='department', user_role='general_manager', and correct entity_id; (g) /api/admin/analysis-doc streams the DOCX with Content-Type application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, PK zip magic bytes, size 42,503 bytes, Content-Disposition attachment filename=GCAAN_ACTIVT_Analysis.docx; (h) regression on /departments, /activities, /reports/summary, /admin/system-info, /admin/backup — all still 200 with expected shapes. limit query bounds enforced (0 and 1001 both 422). No mocked APIs. No frontend testing per scope.

minor.issue: date_from/date_to are compared as raw ISO strings against the stored 'at' field (also ISO string), so lexical ordering happens to work for UTC ISO-8601 with the same offset ('+00:00') but will silently mis-filter if a client passes date-only strings (e.g. '2026-01-15') or a different timezone. Consider parsing to datetime and normalizing to UTC. Not blocking.

login audit uses entity_type='auth' rather than 'login'. Filter by action=login works, but review_request wording ('entity_type=login') would fail. Filter by entity_type=auth is used in the test suite to match the actual implementation.

action_items: Optional: normalize date_from/date_to in list_audit_logs (server.py:1097-1103) — parse via datetime.fromisoformat and coerce to UTC before comparing so that partial or TZ-different inputs are handled correctly.
Optional: consider indexing db.audit_logs on {at:-1} and {action:1, at:-1} for faster filter/summary queries as the collection grows (Motor create_index in seed_initial_data).

Optional: analysis-doc endpoint reads the entire DOCX into memory before streaming (server.py:1142-1144). For 42 KB it's harmless, but wrap in FileResponse or use a chunked read if the file ever grows large.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:47-72 log_audit — properly async, uses uuid4 for id, ISO timestamp, and swallows exceptions with a warning so audit failure never breaks the primary flow. Good.

server.py:222-235 /auth/login — logs both failed (status='failed', synthetic user with just username) and successful (real user) attempts. Failed-login user_id is None which correctly represents the anonymous/unknown case.

server.py:1079-1106 GET /admin/audit-logs — GM-guarded via require_roles, correct pydantic Query bounds (limit 1..1000), _id excluded via projection, sorted by at desc. Returns {total,count,logs} matching spec.
server.py:1109-1133 GET /admin/audit-logs/summary — total, last_24h (via ISO string comparison since 'at' is stored as ISO string), failed_logins_24h, by_action aggregation. All correct.
server.py:1136-1149 GET /admin/analysis-doc — GM-guarded, 404 if missing, correct DOCX media-type, attachment Content-Disposition. File exists at /app/docs/GCAAN_ACTIVT_Analysis.docx (42,503 B).
seed_data_creation: test_audit_and_analysis.py creates fixture user TEST_audit_dm_probe (department_manager, password pw1234) idempotently for non-GM 403 checks and one temporary TEST_audit_dept_<ts> department that is created→updated→deleted within a single test to verify audit rows. All test artifacts are prefixed TEST_ for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 covers audit logs + analysis-doc endpoints. Full suite is now 118/118. Note: audit rows for logins use entity_type='auth' (not 'login'); filter by action='login' is the reliable way to isolate them. Any future test that queries date_from/date_to must pass full ISO-8601 UTC strings (the endpoint does a lexical string compare against the stored 'at' field). Test creds unchanged: admin/admin123. TEST_audit_dm_probe (pw1234) is reusable across reruns.
